definitions.text: Something that happens to someone by chance, a chance occurrence.
examples: The raffle is just a matter of luck.
Gilbert had some bad luck yesterday — he got pick-pocketed and lost fifty dollars.
Sometimes it takes a bit of luck to get success.
I couldn't believe my luck when I found a fifty dollar bill on the street.

definitions.text: A superstitious feeling that brings fortune or success.
examples: He blew on the dice for luck.
I wish you lots of luck for the exam tomorrow.

definitions.text: To find something through good fortune; used with into, on, onto or upon.
